confirm_delete_policy: Are you sure you want to delete this policy?
policy_migration_desc: Create a background task that copies stored objects from the source policy to the target policy. File records are updated during migration.
policy_migration_same_policy_error: Source and target policies must be different.
policy_migration_dry_run_ready: The target policy passed preflight checks. Review the plan before creating the task.
policy_migration_dry_run_blocked: The migration cannot start until the reported issues are resolved.
policy_migration_warning_target_capacity: Target free capacity cannot be verified by this storage driver. Confirm the target has enough capacity before creating the migration task.
policy_capacity_unsupported_desc: Underlying storage capacity is not exposed by this driver.
policy_capacity_unavailable_desc: Underlying storage capacity is not available right now.
policy_driver_options_load_failed: Storage driver options could not be loaded. Refresh and try again.
delete_policy_desc: This will not delete stored files, but new uploads using this policy will fail.
force_delete_policy_desc: This policy is still referenced by unfinished uploads. Continuing will clean up those upload sessions and temporary objects; deletion will still be rejected if stored file blobs use this policy.
s3_path_style_desc: When enabled, requests use /bucket/key URLs for MinIO, RustFS, and similar compatible services. Services that support virtual-hosted-style addressing can usually disable this and use bucket.endpoint/key URLs.
provider_resumable_upload_strategy_serve: The browser uploads to AsterDrive, which writes the file to Microsoft Graph. This is the default and most compatible path.
provider_resumable_upload_strategy_front: The browser uploads sequential ranges through a temporary Graph upload URL, so AsterDrive does not carry the file bytes. Microsoft must allow CORS preflight for PUT and Content-Range; use server relay when it does not.
s3_endpoint_hint: Enter the S3-compatible API endpoint and keep the bucket in the bucket field. Providers differ on path-style support, so test the connection before saving.
cos_endpoint_hint: For Tencent COS, use the bucket domain such as https://<bucket-appid>.cos.<region>.myqcloud.com; enter the bucket separately, for example <bucket-appid>.
azure_blob_endpoint_hint: Enter the Azure Blob service endpoint, for example https://<account>.blob.core.windows.net. Use the bucket field for the container name.
sftp_endpoint_hint: Enter an SFTP server endpoint such as sftp://example.com:22. Put the remote root directory in base path.
sftp_host_key_fingerprint_hint: Run the connection test once. If the host key is unknown, confirm the reported SHA256 fingerprint here before saving.
s3_endpoint_protocol_required_error: S3 endpoint must include http:// or https://.
azure_blob_endpoint_protocol_required_er: Azure Blob endpoint must include http:// or https://.
sftp_endpoint_protocol_required_error: SFTP endpoint must use sftp:// or omit the scheme.
download_strategy_relay_stream_desc: AsterDrive fetches the object from the storage backend and streams it back to the browser. Use this when you need the app node to fully control response headers, same-origin delivery, or downstream network policy.
download_strategy_presigned_desc: After AsterDrive completes permission checks, it redirects the browser to a short-lived GET URL from the storage backend. This reduces app-node download bandwidth, but the backend serves the final response and cache behavior.
upload_strategy_relay_stream_desc: The browser uploads to AsterDrive, and the server relays the bytes directly to the storage backend. The normal path does not write local temporary files; only a small fallback path uses temp files. relay_stream does not perform SHA256 deduplication.
upload_strategy_presigned_desc: Upload directly to the storage backend via presigned URLs. Files ≤ chunk size use a single PUT; larger files use multipart direct upload with resume support. This path does not perform SHA256 deduplication and requires the backend to expose the `ETag` response header.
local_content_dedup_desc: When enabled, local uploads compute SHA-256 after the temporary file is written and reuse blobs with identical content. When disabled, uploads skip the extra full-file read and always create a new blob.
storage_native_processing_enabled_desc: Applies only to this storage policy. Allow it to call the storage provider's native data processing features; for Tencent COS this means COS CI and may incur cloud-provider charges. AsterDrive caches generated thumbnails and similar derivatives so they are not processed on every view.
storage_native_thumbnail_extensions_desc: Per-policy suffix list, comma-separated, for example jpg, jpeg, png, webp, gif. Only matching suffixes try storage-native thumbnails; other files continue through the global media processors.
storage_native_media_metadata_enabled_de: Allow this policy to call the provider's media-info parser. On Tencent COS this uses COS CI GetMediainfo and is charged by request count; an empty suffix list will not trigger native parsing.
storage_native_media_metadata_extensions: Per-policy audio/video suffix list, comma-separated. Only add suffixes you want the storage backend to parse; leaving this empty keeps the switch on but processes no files.
max_file_size_desc: 0 means unlimited. Uploads larger than this value are rejected.
chunk_size_desc: Files larger than this are split into chunks. For object-storage relay_stream and presigned modes, and for remote presigned mode, this also controls the multipart part size; object-storage multipart parts are normalized to at least 5 MiB. 0 = always use single upload.
policy_test_failed_confirm_desc: The current storage connection failed the connection test. You can still save it, but uploads using this policy may fail. Do you want to continue?
policy_force_deleted: Policy deleted and unfinished uploads cleaned up
policy_cos_cors_confirm_desc: This writes an AsterDrive CORS rule to the Tencent COS bucket with every origin from the system public_site_url setting. AsterDrive preserves other rules and replaces only the rule whose ID is asterdrive-presigned-access.
policy_cos_cors_desc: Presigned upload or download makes the browser talk to COS directly, so the bucket must expose response headers such as ETag, Range, and request IDs.
policy_cos_cors_origins: AllowedOrigin values come from every configured public_site_url origin.
policy_cos_cors_relay_desc: This policy currently uses server relay, which usually does not need browser CORS on COS. Configure this now if you plan to switch to presigned mode.
policy_cos_cors_uses_draft: This will use the endpoint, bucket, and credentials currently in the form.
policy_cos_cors_uses_saved: This will use the saved policy endpoint, bucket, and credentials.
policy_s3_driver_promotion_confirm_desc: AsterDrive will only change this saved policy from generic S3 to {{driver}}. It will not copy objects, change the bucket, change the base path, or enable storage-native processing options.
policy_s3_driver_promotion_desc: The current S3 configuration matches {{driver}}. After the policy is saved, promote it in place when the bucket and base path already contain the existing objects.
policy_s3_driver_promotion_unsaved_block: Save or discard current edits before promoting the saved policy.
policy_s3_driver_suggestion_desc: This endpoint appears to belong to {{driver}}. Use the dedicated driver for provider-specific validation, safer defaults, and future service capabilities while keeping the same S3-compatible storage layout.
storage_policy_assignments_desc: Choose the default policy group for this user's uploads. Only one group can be bound at a time.
initial_policy_delete_blocked: The built-in policy with ID 1 cannot be deleted.
default_policy_required: A default policy must always exist while assignments remain.
default_policy_remove_blocked: Set another policy as default before removing the current default policy.
policy_wizard_step_storage_desc: Pick the storage backend for this policy.
policy_wizard_step_connection_desc: Review the connection settings required by this storage backend.
policy_wizard_step_object_storage_connec: Set the object-storage endpoint, bucket, and credentials.
policy_wizard_step_sftp_desc: Set the SFTP endpoint, SSH username, password, and remote root path.
policy_wizard_step_tencent_cos_connectio: Set the Tencent COS bucket domain, bucket, and credentials.
policy_wizard_step_azure_blob_connection: Set the Azure Blob endpoint, container name, storage account name, and key.
policy_wizard_step_onedrive_desc: Choose the Microsoft Graph cloud, then authorize with Microsoft sign-in after saving.
policy_wizard_step_remote_desc: Choose the remote node that will store objects for this policy.
policy_wizard_step_local_desc: Name the policy and choose the local storage path.
policy_wizard_step_rules_desc: Adjust upload behavior and verify the final policy settings.
policy_wizard_choose_driver_desc: Start with where this policy stores uploads. You can refine connection details and limits in the next steps.
policy_wizard_local_storage_desc: Store files on the AsterDrive server filesystem. Simple setup and suitable for single-node deployments.
policy_wizard_remote_storage_desc: Store files on another AsterDrive node over the internal remote storage protocol. Good for tiered or federated deployments.
policy_wizard_s3_storage_desc: Store files in an S3-compatible object bucket such as Amazon S3, MinIO, or RustFS.
policy_wizard_sftp_storage_desc: Store files on an SFTP server through server-side streaming. Browsers never connect to SFTP directly.
policy_wizard_tencent_cos_storage_desc: Store files in Tencent Cloud COS and enable storage-native document preview through COS CI.
policy_wizard_azure_blob_storage_desc: Store files in an Azure Blob container with native SAS URLs and Block Blob multipart uploads.
policy_wizard_onedrive_storage_desc: Store files in OneDrive, SharePoint document libraries, or Microsoft 365 group drives through Microsoft Graph.
policy_wizard_bucket_required: Bucket is required for object storage policies.
policy_wizard_container_required: Container is required for Azure Blob storage policies.
policy_wizard_remote_node_required: Choose a remote node before continuing.
policy_wizard_remote_storage_target_requ: Choose a remote storage target before continuing.
policy_wizard_remote_nodes_empty: Create and configure at least one remote node before saving a remote storage policy.
remote_storage_targets_empty: Create a remote storage target for this node before saving the policy.
remote_storage_targets_load_failed: Failed to load remote storage targets.
remote_storage_target_drivers_load_faile: Failed to load remote storage target drivers.
policy_remote_storage_targets_view_title: Storage targets on the selected remote node
policy_remote_storage_targets_view_desc: These targets belong to the remote node capability. Choose the target this policy should use; adjust targets from the remote node page.
policy_wizard_object_storage_helper: Connection tests and upload strategy are available after the basic connection is filled in.
policy_wizard_tencent_cos_helper: Connection tests and upload strategy are available after the basic connection is filled in. COS CI preview links are signed by the backend per object.
policy_wizard_azure_blob_helper: Connection tests and upload strategy are available after the basic connection is filled in. Large files map to Azure Block Blob uploads.
policy_wizard_onedrive_helper: Enter the Microsoft application client ID while creating the policy. After saving, the dialog switches to editing so you can authorize Microsoft Graph directly. Choose Global for microsoft.com tenants and China for 21Vianet tenants.
policy_wizard_remote_helper: The selected remote node handles network transport. This policy controls the remote path prefix, upload mode, and size limits.
policy_wizard_local_helper: Use a relative or absolute path. Leave it empty to fall back to the application's default data directory.
policy_wizard_sftp_helper: AsterDrive streams uploads and downloads through the app server, then reads and writes files over SFTP using the configured SSH credentials.
policy_wizard_remote_rules_helper: Remote storage does not use per-policy endpoint or credentials. Bind a remote node, choose pure stream relay or presigned direct upload, then configure upload limits and default-policy behavior.
policy_wizard_onedrive_rules_helper: OneDrive upload behavior is managed by Microsoft Graph upload sessions. Configure size limits and default-policy behavior here; OAuth authorization happens after the policy is saved.
policy_wizard_summary_desc: Review the key settings before saving. You can still go back and adjust any step.
policy_editor_overview_desc: Update the policy name, storage path, and review the active storage driver.
policy_editor_connection_desc: Adjust the endpoint, bucket, and credentials. Leaving secret fields empty keeps the current values.
policy_editor_remote_desc: Bind this policy to a configured remote node. Upload behavior and size limits are configured separately below.
policy_editor_onedrive_desc: Configure the target drive and authorize the Microsoft app used by this storage policy.
policy_storage_native_section_desc: Control whether this policy can call storage-backend data processing features, and which suffixes can use native thumbnails or native media-info parsing.
policy_editor_rules_desc: Configure upload behavior, file size limits, and whether this should be the default policy.
policy_editor_summary_desc: Review the current configuration before saving.
policy_edit_context_local_desc: Local policies write directly to the server filesystem. Adjust paths and upload rules below.
policy_edit_context_object_storage_desc: Test object storage connections before saving. Blank secret fields keep the current credentials.
policy_edit_context_sftp_desc: SFTP policies stream through the app server and write to the configured remote root. Blank password fields keep the current credential.
policy_edit_context_azure_blob_desc: Azure Blob policies use the storage account key to issue short-lived SAS URLs. Test the connection before saving; blank secret fields keep the current credentials.
policy_edit_context_remote_desc: Remote policies transfer through the bound node. Adjust paths, node binding, and upload rules below.
policy_edit_context_onedrive_desc: OneDrive policies use saved Microsoft Graph OAuth credentials. Save target changes before starting authorization.
onedrive_cloud_desc: Global uses graph.microsoft.com. China uses microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn. Personal Microsoft accounts must use Global.
onedrive_setup_notice_redirect_uri: The redirect URI in Entra ID must exactly match the URI shown here.
onedrive_setup_notice_permissions: Use Microsoft Graph delegated permissions for the app registration.
onedrive_setup_notice_cloud: Keep the app registration, sign-in endpoint, and Graph endpoint in the same cloud. Do not mix Global and China.
onedrive_setup_notice_personal_china: Personal Microsoft accounts do not support the China endpoint. Use Global instead.
onedrive_target_summary_auto: Resolve the default drive after authorization
onedrive_account_mode_desc: This controls how AsterDrive resolves the target drive in Microsoft Graph.
onedrive_tenant_mode_desc: By default, Microsoft sign-in supports both personal Microsoft accounts and Entra ID organization accounts. Change this only to restrict sign-in.
onedrive_tenant_custom_desc: Enter a tenant ID or a verified tenant domain.
onedrive_drive_id_desc: Usually leave this empty. AsterDrive resolves the default drive after Microsoft sign-in. Fill it only to target a non-default document library or override automatic resolution.
onedrive_root_item_id_desc: Optional. Leave empty or use root to store under the drive root.
onedrive_site_id_desc: Required for SharePoint site drive mode unless a drive ID is provided.
onedrive_group_id_desc: Required for Microsoft 365 group drive mode unless a drive ID is provided.
onedrive_client_id_placeholder: Application (client) ID from the Microsoft app registration
onedrive_client_id_keep_placeholder: Leave empty to use the saved client ID
onedrive_client_id_required: Enter the Microsoft application client ID before starting authorization.
onedrive_client_secret_placeholder: Client secret value from the Microsoft app registration
onedrive_client_secret_keep_placeholder: Leave empty to use the saved client secret
onedrive_client_secret_required: Enter the Microsoft application client secret before starting authorization.
onedrive_credential_reauth_required_desc: Thumbnail generation, capacity checks, and background read tasks can fail until this policy is reauthorized.
onedrive_credential_reason_reauth_requir: The Microsoft Graph credential is no longer usable. Reauthorize this storage policy.
onedrive_credential_reason_missing_refre: No refresh token is available. Reauthorize Microsoft Graph.
onedrive_credential_reason_invalid_grant: Microsoft rejected the refresh token. The grant may be revoked, expired, or blocked by account policy.
onedrive_credential_reason_invalid_clien: The Microsoft app configuration is invalid. Check the Client ID, Secret, and redirect URI, then reauthorize.
onedrive_credential_reason_missing_acces: The Microsoft token response did not include an access token. Check the app configuration and reauthorize.
onedrive_credential_reason_drive_resolut: The credential works, but AsterDrive could not resolve the target OneDrive. Check the account type and target settings.
onedrive_credential_desc_authorized: A saved credential is attached to this policy. Validate it after changing drive settings or Microsoft permissions.
onedrive_credential_desc_missing: Authorize Microsoft Graph before using this policy for uploads.
onedrive_redirect_uri_desc: Use this exact redirect URI in the Entra ID app registration. It must match before Microsoft sign-in can complete.
onedrive_save_before_authorize: Save OneDrive policy changes before starting authorization.
onedrive_save_before_validate: Save OneDrive policy changes before validating the credential.
onedrive_authorization_failed: OneDrive authorization failed. Reauthorize Microsoft Graph.
onedrive_authorization_failed_invalid_st: The authorization request expired. Start OneDrive authorization again.
onedrive_authorization_failed_provider: Microsoft returned an authorization error. Check the app configuration and reauthorize.
onedrive_authorization_failed_token_exch: Could not exchange the Microsoft Graph access token. Check the Client ID, Secret, and redirect URI.
onedrive_authorization_failed_drive_reso: Authorization returned, but the OneDrive target could not be resolved. Check the account type and target settings.
onedrive_authorization_failed_unsupporte: This storage authorization provider is not supported. Check the Microsoft Graph provider configuration.
onedrive_authorization_failed_invalid_re: The OneDrive authorization callback was invalid. Reauthorize Microsoft Graph.
onedrive_authorization_failed_server: The server failed while completing OneDrive authorization. Try again later.
policy_onedrive_created_authorize_next: OneDrive policy created. Authorize Microsoft Graph next.
